facts.claim: 29 rules: count->color threshold engine (VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO + frontend-only LARANJA), multi-layer bed/sector rollups repeating VERMELHO>AMARELO>NEUTRO, assistido attendance+audit workflow, hardcoded-VERMELHO system dispatch, v1->v3 trilha migration with dead legacy branches, duplicated frontend palettes.
No rule cites a published clinical reference; all logic is internal severity-aggregation over criteria computed in other clusters (sepse, ventilacao, sedacao, estabilidade).
define_tipo_alerta (RULE-ALERTAS-003) per-model {amarelo,vermelho} count thresholds: trilha1/2/3/4/8/9 + all v3 + *_Tasy = {2,3}; trilha5/6 v3 = {3,4}; trilha7 v3 = {4,5}.

Histogram: status OK=26, DISCREPANCY=1 (003), AMBIGUOUS=2 (014,025). Type: decision-tree=15, workflow=6, threshold=3, scoring=3, formula=1, validation=1. Verdict: UNVERIFIABLE=2 (001,002), NOT_APPLICABLE=27.
RULE-ALERTAS-001: contar_qtd_criterios_alerta counts esta_alerta==1; verdict UNVERIFIABLE (generic counter, no clinical reference).
003: dead-zone bug — counts strictly between amarelo/vermelho fall to NEUTRO instead of AMARELO; masked only because every shipped model sets vermelho==amarelo+1.
RULE-ALERTAS-006: get_alerta_leito — interactive-sepsis LARANJA override takes top priority over VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO for stored bed color.
RULE-ALERTAS-009: an all-NEUTRO bed is always NOT attended (assistido=False) regardless of pathway data.

014: verdict AMBIGUOUS — whitelist-filters RED criterio messages for 'automatica' beds, dropping non-whitelisted content; contains disabled/commented sepsis-specific message of unclear intentionality.
RULE-ALERTAS-016: dedup logic for repeat VERMELHO notifications exists but is dead — only called from commented-out code.
018/019: system Observacao always hardcodes alerta='VERMELHO'; auto-provisions singleton 'sistema' user with plaintext-looking password literal 'sis_info' — security flag, not clinical.
RULE-ALERTAS-020: NEUTRO-reset of assistido=False on save() applied inconsistently — present trilha3/5/6/7/9+v3, absent v1 trilha1/2/4/8.
RULE-ALERTAS-021: v1->v3 migration in progress — TrilhaSepseV3/ProfilaxiaV3 active; legacy TrilhaSEPSEModel/TrilhaProfilaxiasModel imported but commented out (dead).

RULE-ALERTAS-024 statusTrilha: NEUTRO(green)/VERMELHO(red)/AMARELO(yellow)/LARANJA(orange)/ASSISTIDO(blue); inconsistent hex preserved verbatim (LARANJA.ballBackground #712B08 vs backgroundShade base #713708).
027/028: two independent sector alert-count KPIs (keyed on get_payload()['alerta'] vs alerta_nao_assistido) both use VERMELHO>AMARELO>NEUTRO but source different fields; not confirmed identical.
No unit/window-bug patterns here. Dead code: 005 (superseded), 014 (commented message), 016 (dead call site), 021 (commented legacy models).

Feeds: early-warning-scores (threshold engine), sepsis (LARANJA override + disabled message), platform (sector KPI rollups), governance (audit trail, plaintext-password flag), experience (frontend palettes), retire-candidate (dead branches).

PT-BR vocab: colors VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO/LARANJA/ASSISTIDO; trilha names sepse/ventilacao/sedacao/estabilidade/antimicrobiano/nutricao/equilibrio/profilaxias/eficiencia/auditoria; assistido/assistido_por/assistido_em; status salvo/liberado/inativo; msg 'Risco para SEPSE: realizar abertura do Protocolo Institucional'.

constraints.text: MUST NOT port RULE-ALERTAS-003's dead-zone branch as-is; correct the gap rather than replicate it.
MUST NOT port plaintext-password system-user provisioning (018/019) into v2 auth.
RULE-ALERTAS-014's disabled sepsis message MUST go to RATIFY, not silent ADOPT/RETIRE.

open_questions: Was RULE-ALERTAS-014's sepsis message intentionally retired or accidentally disabled?
Should 027/028 sector alert-count KPIs reconcile into one v2 source, or stay distinct?
